rect.data-narration: Connection-Oriented Transport (COT) is a transport-layer protocol that establishes a connection between a sender and a receiver before data is sent.

circle.data-narration: The sender initiates the connection by sending a connection request to the receiver.

line.data-narration: The receiver accepts the connection request and responds with a connection acknowledgement.

rect.data-narration: Once the connection is established, the sender and receiver can start sending data to each other.

line.data-narration: The sender breaks the connection after data transmission is complete.

rect.data-narration: Connectionless Transport (CCL) is a transport-layer protocol that does not establish a connection before data transmission.

circle.data-narration: Each data packet is sent independently and does not guarantee reliable delivery.

line.data-narration: The receiver receives the data packet and acknowledges its receipt

rect.data-narration: There is no guarantee of reliable, error-free delivery of data.

line.data-narration: The sender and receiver do not maintain a connection during transmission.

text.data-narration: Example: Sending a file from a sender machine to a receiver machine using COT
